Author: Jamie Wright
Description:
As per accepted defect quote QD-1576 dated 14-10-2025.
Following completion of the five-yearly inspection and testing of the fire detection and alarm system at 54–72 Kingsgrove Road, Belmore, please see a detailed summary of our findings below.
1. System Performance (Compliant Items)
•	EWIS / Speaker Testing:
All sound pressure level (SPL) readings recorded between 74 dB and 96 dB, which is compliant with audibility requirements under AS 1670.4, ensuring adequate occupant warning levels.

•	Fire Indicator Panel (FIP):
o	All voltages and circuit resistances tested within acceptable limits.
o	System integrity confirmed with no major faults present.
o	All speakers and warning systems operated as intended.

•	Overall System Condition:
The system is generally in good working order, with the majority of devices responding correctly during testing.
2. Non-Compliances & Defects Identified
The following items require attention to maintain compliance with AS 1851 (Routine Service of Fire Protection Systems) and AS 1670.1 (Fire Detection Systems):
•	Reception Area:
o	Detector 43 displaying Fault F08 (Dirty Head)
o	This indicates contamination affecting detector sensitivity
o	Action: Replace detector to restore compliance
•	Café Area:
o	Multiple detectors non-responsive during functional testing
o	Refer to photos provided
•	Hallway Areas:
o	Detectors not responding to test stimulus- Photos issued for reference
•	Male & Female Bathrooms (End of Hallway):
o	Detectors non-responsive
•	Male Bathrooms (Adjacent to Offices):
o	Detectors on both sides not responding
•	Luxury Car Wash / Warehouse (Toilet Area):
o	Detector non-responsive
•	Office Areas:
o	Several detectors non-responsive, as per photos issued
Note: All above non-responsive devices are considered non-compliant under AS 1851, as detectors must operate correctly under test conditions. (photos sent)
3. Coverage Observations (Non-Compliance Risk)
•	We identified areas between factory walkways with no detection coverage.
•	This may not comply with AS 1670.1 Clause requirements for detector spacing and coverage, depending on building classification and fire engineering design.
Recommendation:
Review these areas for additional detector installation to ensure full coverage and compliance.
4. Summary
Overall, the system performed well with:
•	Strong EWIS performance
•	Compliant panel readings
•	Majority of devices operating correctly
However, the non-responsive detectors and identified gaps in coverage should be rectified to ensure:
•	Ongoing compliance with AS 1851
•	System reliability in the event of an emergency
•	Reduced risk exposure for the building
5. Recommended Next Steps
We recommend:
•	Replacement of faulty/contaminated detectors
•	Investigation and rectification of non-responsive devices
•	Review and possible upgrade of detection coverage in walkway areas
Property: Pickles Auctions - 54 & 68-72 Kingsgrove Road, Belmore, NSW 2192
Task Name: 5 Yearly Detection and Warning System (Quote QD-1576)

Description:
Scope of Works:
Attend site to replace faulty components within the EWIS, program replacement components, and perform testing to verify correct system operation.

Work Performed:

Attended site as scheduled to carry out required service works on the EWIS.

Replaced electronic chips on the main EWIS unit and the sub EWIS unit due to identified faults.

Programmed the newly installed chips to suit the system configuration and ensure compatibility with existing EWIS hardware and software.

Verified correct programming parameters in accordance with manufacturer specifications and system requirements.

Testing and Commissioning:

Conducted comprehensive functional testing of the EWIS following chip replacement and programming.

Tested system operation to confirm correct communication between the main EWIS and sub EWIS units.

Verified correct activation, system responses, and overall performance to ensure the system operates as intended.

Results:

EWIS found to be operating correctly at the completion of works.

All replaced components function as expected.

System confirmed compliant with operational requirements at the time of testing.

Conclusion:
The EWIS was successfully repaired, programmed, and tested. The system is operational and performing correctly with no further faults identified during this visit.

Unit 15 had set off a heat detector in their kitchen. This did not send an alarm to the ASE. Investigated problem with ASE and monitoring. Main FIP Notifier 2800 system normal.

One fault and one isolate on AMPAC Sub FIP see photos attached.

Material used: 1x reconditioned FRM-1 relay module.
